title: Real Estate CRMs in 2026: Pricing, Trade-offs, and Who Actually Wins
description: Follow Up Boss, Lofty, kvCORE, and Sierra Interactive compared on pricing, integrations, and trade-offs for solo agents and teams.
tldr: Follow Up Boss wins for simplicity and open integrations. Sierra Interactive offers the best value for small teams scaling up. Lofty bundles lead generation but pricing is opaque. kvCORE requires direct vendor contact for current plans.

body.text: Real estate CRMs split into two camps: lead engines that bundle advertising, and pure workflow tools that connect to your existing stack. Follow Up Boss and Sierra Interactive prioritize integration depth and agent productivity. Lofty sells an all-in-one platform with built-in ad management. kvCORE sits behind Inside Real Estate's paywall with no public pricing. We reviewed current pricing pages and feature lists to surface the trade-offs.

We pulled pricing directly from vendor sites in May 2026. No invented test calls or fabricated closing counts. We mapped published plan tiers, seat limits, and feature gates. Where pricing was unavailable, we note it. Integration claims come from vendor marketing pages. Public customer testimonials appear where vendors surface them.

Follow Up Boss sells workflow simplicity and 250+ integrations. The vendor markets to solo agents and teams who want a clean CRM that connects to any lead source. AI features like smart summaries and predictive lead scoring ship standard. Calling is an add-on for the entry tier.

body.name: Follow Up Boss
body.price: $69/month Grow plan (add calling for $39/user), $499/month Pro for up to 10 users, $1,000/month Platform for up to 30 users
pros: 250+ integrations including Zillow, email-to-lead tools, and calendar sync
AI features (smart summaries, suggested tasks, predictive lead prioritization) included in all plans
7-day-a-week phone and email support with no contract
Smart Lists and Action Plans automate follow-up without custom workflows
Annual billing saves two months (Grow $58/month, Pro $416/month)
cons: Calling costs extra on Grow plan ($39/user monthly, $33/user annually)
Team inboxes and call reporting locked to Pro tier and above
No built-in lead generation; bring your own sources
Additional users on Pro cost $49/month, $41/month annual

body.text: Lofty (formerly Chime) bundles CRM, IDX websites, dialer, and paid lead generation into tiered packages. The vendor lists four plans—Agent, Team, Broker, Enterprise—but pricing requires a demo request. Feature descriptions mention AI assistants (Sales Agent, Social Agent, Homeowner Agent) and over 33 lead generation methods including Google PPC, direct mail, and Lofty Bloom for hyper-local leads with zip exclusivity.

body.name: Lofty
body.price: See vendor for current pricing (Agent, Team, Broker, Enterprise tiers; ad campaigns priced separately)
pros: Integrated lead generation: Google PPC, Facebook ads, direct mail, Google LSA, and Lofty Bloom
AI agents for 24/7 lead capture (Sales Agent), social posting (Social Agent), and database mining (Homeowner Agent)
Transaction management and back-office services included at higher tiers
Social Studio automates social media marketing
Closely mobile app for home search experience
cons: No transparent pricing; all plans require demo request
Ad campaigns add 20% management fee; Home Evaluation Page costs $70/month if no IDX site
Lead estimates (20-30 buyer leads, 10-20 seller leads) are historical averages, not guarantees
White-label and advanced routing available only on Broker/Enterprise tiers
Lender co-op splits marketing cost but limits to Accelerator and Enterprise plans

body.text: kvCORE, owned by Inside Real Estate, offers CRM, IDX websites, and behavior-based lead scoring. No pricing page was accessible in the research bundle. The platform is known for MLS integration depth and broker-level tools, but current plan details require direct vendor contact.

body.name: kvCORE
pros: Deep MLS integration and behavioral lead scoring
Broker-friendly tools for managing large agent networks
cons: No public pricing or feature comparison available
Requires demo to see plan tiers and seat costs

body.text: Sierra Interactive sells three tiers: Starter for solo agents, Essential for evolving teams, and Growth for high-volume operations. Plans include users, IDX website tiers (Ace, Pro, Exec, Luxury, Pinnacle), and integrations. The vendor waives setup fees on annual contracts. Additional users scale down in cost as team size grows.

body.name: Sierra Interactive
body.price: $299.95/month Starter (1 user, annual), $399.95/month Essential (3 users), $599.95/month Growth (5 users); monthly pricing adds $60-125/month and $500 setup fee
pros: Transparent per-user pricing: $75/user (Starter), $60/user for 4-10 on Essential, $25/user for 11+ on Essential, down to $5/user for 50+ on Growth
No setup fee on annual plans; $500 one-time on monthly
Website options scale by plan: Ace/Pro on Starter, add Exec/Luxury/Pinnacle on Essential/Growth
Growth tier includes 90-day onboarding, two hours additional training, dedicated support
MLS feeds: one included, $25/additional on all tiers
cons: Monthly pricing expensive: Starter $359.95, Essential $474.95, Growth $724.95 plus $500 setup
Essential and Growth tiers required for premium website designs (Exec, Luxury, Pinnacle)
Agent Site Manager costs extra ($50/month) on Starter
30-day onboarding on Starter vs. 60-90 days on higher plans
Feature list on pricing page cuts off mid-sentence; full comparison unclear

items: Solo agents who value simplicity and integration breadth: Follow Up Boss Grow plan ($69/month, add calling if needed). Seven-day support, no contract, AI features standard.
Small teams (3-5 agents) scaling up: Sierra Interactive Essential ($399.95/month annual, 3 users). Transparent per-seat pricing, included MLS feed, 60-day onboarding.
Teams buying leads and want bundled advertising: Lofty Team or Broker tier. Request pricing; expect ad management fees and lender co-op options.
High-volume teams (10+ agents) needing priority support: Follow Up Boss Pro ($499/month for 10 users) or Sierra Interactive Growth ($599.95/month for 5 users, $10-25/user for 6-49 additional).
Brokers managing large agent networks: kvCORE if MLS depth is critical; request demo for current pricing.
Follow Up Boss Grow without calling add-on if you need AI features that rely on call data. The vendor notes AI results improve with calling enabled.
Lofty ad campaigns if you already run your own Google/Facebook ads. The 20% management fee and opaque lead estimates make ROI hard to forecast.
Sierra Interactive monthly billing. The $60-125/month premium over annual pricing plus $500 setup fee makes little sense unless you're testing short-term.
kvCORE if you need transparent pricing up front. The lack of a public pricing page adds friction to budgeting.
